title: meconopsis integrifolia subsp. souliei
intro: Meconopsis integrifolia subsp. souliei, also known as the Yunnan Blue Poppy, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant belonging to the poppy family. Traditionally, it has been used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its alkaloid compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Meconopsis integrifolia subsp. souliei are often used in infusions or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
